In the diagrams in the Ball Tosser report, three people threw a ball with different amounts of force. The person who used the most force caused the ball to:

[{'answer_text': 'travel the shortest distance.', 'text': 'travel the shortest distance.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'travel the same distance as the others.', 'text': 'travel the same distance as the others.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'travel the farthest and highest.', 'text': 'travel the farthest and highest.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'land behind them.', 'text': 'land behind them.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'move in a perfect circle.', 'text': 'move in a perfect circle.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'stop in the middle of the air.', 'text': 'stop in the middle of the air.', 'weight': 0}]
travel the same distance as the others.
stop in the middle of the air.

For a science experiment, why is a launching machine often better than a person throwing a ball?

[{'answer_text': 'A person is usually much stronger.', 'text': 'A person is usually much stronger.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'A machine provides a more consistent, repeatable throw.', 'text': 'A machine provides a more consistent, repeatable throw.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'A person cannot throw a ball very far.', 'text': 'A person cannot throw a ball very far.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': "A machine can change the ball's color.", 'text': "A machine can change the ball's color.", 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'A machine costs less than paying a person.', 'text': 'A machine costs less than paying a person.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'A person might get tired after one throw.', 'text': 'A person might get tired after one throw.', 'weight': 0}]
A person is usually much stronger.
A machine provides a more consistent, repeatable throw.
A person cannot throw a ball very far.
A machine can change the ball's color.
A machine costs less than paying a person.
A person might get tired after one throw.
